methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $5M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 11 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Cohesive Robotics is a Seed-stage robotics and manufacturing company founded by David Pietrocola that has raised $5M+. The valuation universe comprises 9 private robotics peers (including 1X Technologies, Divergent, Exotec, Locus Robotics, and Bedrock Robotics) and 1 public comparable (277810.KQ).

The Stage-Progression method is the primary applicable approach, using the $5M total raised as a proxy baseline and applying a 2.5x multiplier typical of Seed-to-next-stage advancement in venture capital. This yields an implied valuation of $12M. A Peer-Median analysis proved inapplicable; while 11 same-sector peers exist, none generated a sufficiently deep subset (≥3 datapoints) when filtered by stage, adjacent stage, or similar valuation tier. Applying an all-sector median would lack specificity.

The triangulation supports a valuation range of $11M to $14M, with a median of $12M. This range reflects material uncertainty: the analysis rests on a single method and lacks granular comparable data within the narrow Seed-stage robotics cohort. Key risks to this valuation include execution risk inherent to early-stage hardware and the absence of revenue metrics or product-market validation indicators in the available data. A subsequent funding round with clearer financial traction or a denser peer dataset would materially improve precision.
